next_steps: Perform ultrasound-guided core needle biopsy of the retroperitoneal or mediastinal soft-tissue mass
Obtain serum IgG4 level, IgG subclass levels, and total serum protein electrophoresis
Perform immunohistochemical staining of biopsy tissue for IgG4-positive plasma cells and IgG4/IgG ratio
Evaluate for diabetes insipidus with serum and urine osmolality, and serum sodium
Assess renal function carefully given the retroperitoneal involvement and hydronephrosis
Consider PET/CT scan to assess total body inflammatory burden
Start high-dose glucocorticoids if infection is ruled out and clinical suspicion for IgG4-RD remains high
Perform image-guided core needle biopsy of the retroperitoneal or mediastinal soft-tissue mass
Request immunohistochemistry of biopsy tissue for CD68, CD1a, and BRAF V600E (to evaluate for ECD)
Request immunohistochemistry of biopsy tissue for IgG4 and IgG to calculate IgG4/IgG plasma cell ratio
Order serum and urine osmolality, and serum sodium levels to evaluate for diabetes insipidus
Perform PET/CT scan to assess total body inflammatory burden
Initiate high-dose glucocorticoids as bridge therapy pending definitive biopsy results
Consult Hematology-Oncology and Urology/Interventional Radiology for definitive biopsy and potential ureteral stenting
